data.narrative:
Strider Technologies is a Series C-stage defense and security company founded by Greg Levesque, Eric Levesque, and Mike Brown that has raised $117M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 11 same-sector and adjacent-stage companies, comprising 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including True Anomaly, Saronic, Chaos Industries, and Anduril Industries. The peer universe ranges from $500M to $9.2B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $117M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C progression, yielding $199M. Peer-median methodology references the median valuation of $1.4B across the 11-company comp set, with a range spanning $500M at the low end to $9.25B at the high end.

The combined analysis supports a valuation range of $169M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median point of $799M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion within the peer set, driven partly by stage heterogeneity—True Anomaly and Vannevar Labs both trade at $1.5B despite differing stages. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, customer concentration, and near-term funding trajectory, which would meaningfully refine the median estimate.
